title: Boat Rental in Mekong Delta: How to Choose the Right Charter in 2026
html:
Exploring the Mekong Delta by boat is the most authentic way to experience its floating markets, winding canals, and stilt villages. Renting a boat in the delta typically costs from EUR 60 for a half-day shared tour to EUR 350 for a private crewed motorboat, and you can compare verified options through BoatHire24.

What types of boats can you rent in the Mekong Delta?

You can rent traditional sampans, modern speedboats, and small cabin cruisers, each suited to different parts of the delta. The labyrinthine waterways around My Tho and Ben Tre are best navigated by small, shallow-draft boats, while the wider stretches near Can Tho can accommodate larger vessels.

Which boat to rent: a quick guide

  1. Sampan (traditional wooden boat), ideal for narrow canals and market visits, seats up to 8 guests, from EUR 60 for 2 hours.
  2. Speedboat (e.g. Olympic 490), best for fast transfers between islands and tours, seats 4 to 6 guests, from EUR 120 half-day. You can check this speedboat on BoatHire24 for exact availability.
  3. Small cabin cruiser (e.g. Lagoon 380), good for overnight trips with 6 guests, from EUR 250 full day.
  4. Crewed motor yacht, suitable for larger groups of 8 to 12 guests, from EUR 400 full day with skipper.
  5. Kayak or paddleboard (add-on rental), often offered as an extra on boat charters for exploring tributaries, from EUR 15 per hour.

Where to set off in the Mekong Delta

Ben Tre Harbour is a popular departure point for tours among the coconut plantations. My Tho Boat Wharf provides quick access to the four islets known as the Unicorn, Phoenix, Dragon, and Tortoise islands. In Can Tho, Ninh Kieu Pier is the main hub for trips to Cai Rang floating market. Vinh Long also has several private jetties where you can hire a sampan. For a more secluded start, consider the Tra On floating market area, accessible from small jetties in the surrounding villages.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in the Mekong Delta?

Prices depend on boat type and duration, ranging from EUR 60 for a basic tour to EUR 500 for a full-day yacht charter with crew. You can book a charter online to compare listings and find your preferred vessel.

  • Shared sampan tour (2, 3 hours): EUR 60, 90 per person
  • Private small motorboat (half-day, 4 hours): EUR 150, 200 for up to 6 guests
  • Catamaran or cabin cruiser (full day, 8 hours): EUR 350, 500 for up to 8 guests
  • Crewed luxury motor yacht (full day, 8 hours): EUR 600, 800 for up to 12 guests

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in the Mekong Delta?

Most rental operators provide a skipper with the charter, so a boating licence is not required. If you wish to drive the boat yourself, you must hold an international certificate of competence (ICC) or a valid Vietnamese boating licence; however, self-drive options are very limited in the delta for safety reasons.

Since the delta is tidal, time your floating market visit for early morning (around 6, 8 am); the current is calmer and the market is most active. Always confirm the tide times with your operator.

At a glance: Mekong Delta boat rental

  • Price band: EUR 60 to EUR 800 per trip
  • Peak months: November to April (dry season)
  • Trip length: 2 hours to multi-day
  • Max guest capacity: 12 on most crewed yachts
  • Local currency accepted: VND, but many operators quote EUR for international travellers

Questions about boat rental in the Mekong Delta

Can I rent a boat for a full day without a skipper?

Bareboat rental is rarely available in the Mekong Delta due to complex waterways and safety regulations; most charters include a local skipper who knows the currents.

Is it better to book a shared tour or a private charter?

A shared tour is more economical (from EUR 60 per person) and sociable, while a private charter offers flexibility and likely a faster vessel for covering longer distances.

What should I bring on a boat trip in the delta?

Sun protection, insect repellent, a hat, and bottled water are essential; a camera is vital for the floating markets, and some operators provide life jackets as standard.

Are there any restrictions for large groups?

Larger groups should book two boats or a crewed catamaran (up to 12 guests); confirm capacity in advance and note that many vessels are licensed for a maximum of 12 passengers under local maritime rules.

Whether you are planning a half-day sampan tour or a week-long expedition, renting a boat through BoatHire24 gives you access to verified operators and transparent pricing in the Mekong Delta. Boat owners are welcome to browse the listings and add your own vessel to the marketplace, extending your reach to international travellers.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Mekong Delta and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
